don: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Doura.
donor: noun. One who makes a donation.
door: noun. A portal of entry into a building, room, or vehicle, typically consisting of a rigid plane movable on a hinge.
dor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dori'o.
dorp: noun. A village or small town; a town considered provincial.
droop: verb. To hang downward; to sag.
drop: noun. A small quantity of liquid, just large enough to hold its own rounded shape through surface tension, especially one that falls from a
dun: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dusun Deyah.
duo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dupaningan Agta.
dup: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Duano.
fon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Fon.
fond: adjective. Having a liking or affection (for). [with of]
fondu: noun. The graded shift from one color into another.
food: noun. Any solid substance that can be consumed by living organisms, especially by eating, in order to sustain life.
fop: noun. A vain man; a dandy.
for: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fore.
ford: noun. A location where a stream is shallow and the bottom has good footing, making it possible to cross from one side to the other with no
found: verb. simple past and past participle of find
four: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 4.
fro: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Old French.
frond: noun. The leaf of a fern, especially a compound leaf.
fud: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for East Futuna.
fun: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fulniô.
fund: noun. A sum or source of money.
fur: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Friulian.
nod: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Northern Thai.
nor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Norwegian.
odor: noun. Any smell, whether fragrant or offensive.
odour: noun. UK standard spelling of odor.
ordo: noun. A musical phrase constructed from one or more statements of one modal pattern and ending in a rest.
oud: noun. A short-necked and fretless plucked stringed instrument of the lute family, of Arab and Turkish origin.
our: determiner. Belonging to us, excluding the person(s) being addressed.
pod: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Ponares.
pond: noun. An inland body of standing water, either natural or man-made, that is smaller than a lake.
pood: noun. An obsolete Russian unit of mass, equal to 40 Russian funt, or about 16.38 kg (approximately 36.11 pounds).
poof: interjection. Onomatopoeia indicating a small explosion with a cloud of smoke; as caused by a deflating object, or a magical disappearance.
poon: noun. Any of several East Indian trees of the genus Calophyllum, yielding a light, hard wood used for masts, spars, etc.
poor: adjective. With no or few possessions or money, particularly in relation to contemporaries who do have them.
pouf: noun. A headdress for women popular in 18th century France.
pound: noun. A unit of weight in various measurement systems.
pour: verb. To cause (liquid, or liquid-like substance) to flow in a stream, either out of a container or into it.
pro: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Old Occitan.
prod: verb. To poke, to push, to touch.
profound: adjective. Descending far below the surface; opening or reaching to great depth; deep.
proof: noun. An effort, process, or operation designed to establish or discover a fact or truth; an act of testing; a test; a trial.
proud: adjective. Feeling honoured (by something); feeling happy or satisfied about an event or fact; gratified.
pud: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Punan Aput.
pun: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Pubian.
pur: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Puruborá.
rod: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rogo.
rondo: noun. A musical composition, commonly of a lively, cheerful character, in which the first strain recurs after each of the other strains.
rood: noun. A crucifix, cross, especially in a church.
roof: noun. The external covering at the top of a building.
run: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Kirundi.
undo: verb. To reverse the effects of an action.
unroof: verb. To remove a roof from, e.g. a building.
updo: noun. A woman's hairdo in which most hair is kept above the neck.
upo: noun. Lagenaria siceraria; a vine grown for its fruit.
upon: preposition. A higher-register or more formal alternative to on in most, though not all, prepositional uses.
urd: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Urdu.
urn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Uruangnirin.
uropod: noun. either of the two posterior abdominal appendages of the lobster, shrimp and some other crustaceans.
